svg.text: Floyd-Warshall — Three Nested Loops, All-Pairs Shortest Paths
"Shortest path from i to j using intermediate vertices in {"{"}1..k{"}"} only."
In place: the third dimension can be dropped because each entry is read before write
in the right order — cleverly, even with the same array.
• Time: O(V³). Space: O(V²) for distance + path-reconstruction matrix.
• Handles negative edges. Detects negative cycles: any d[v][v] < 0 after the loop.
• k loop must be outermost — swapping i or j outside changes correctness.
• Tight, branch-free inner loop is extraordinarily cache-friendly.
• Generalizes to other semirings: transitive closure (∨, ∧), bottleneck (max, min),
count of paths (+, ×) — the algebraic Floyd-Warshall.
Same skeleton, different (⊕, ⊗) operators — the pattern is more general than shortest paths.
